title: High Value Dual Approval Chain
description: A risk-and-compliance control workflow that sequences screening, escalation, and approval gates before transaction progression. Focus case: high value dual approval chain.
assumptions: Reviewer and screening services are available and single-threaded per case where predicates model availability.
Action execution is atomic and enforces predicate preconditions at runtime.
Escalation targets and approver groups are prepopulated and do not change during a case's lifetime.
risks: Delays in manual analyst availability can block progression through screening and approval gates.
Segregation-of-duty conflicts if the same identity exists in multiple approver roles.
False negatives from automated sanction services may permit policy violations to proceed.
Cross-transaction interlocks may create deadlocks if dependencies are cyclic or poorly coordinated.
Insufficient auditability if predicates representing approvals are not persistently logged.
